refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR conflates three distinct functional obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies implementation choice) and focuses on the core problem: making worksite geometry available to downstream planning and execution functions. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent verification and design iteration.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that a worksite spatial model exists in a defined format (e.g., point cloud, mesh, or feature set) and can be queried by planning/execution functions to retrieve feature geometry and attributes within specified accuracy tolerances.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface geometry data from the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and classify static obstacles (e.g., structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and exclusion zones per operational policy.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Integrate terrain, obstacle, and work-zone data into a unified spatial model accessible to planning and execution functions.
refined_fr.note: The parent FR (CR-NGHE-006) remains composite; the sub-FRs are atomic and ready for independent DP and IM assignment.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured geometric model (point clouds, meshes, or voxel grids) with semantic labels and metadata; expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign each zone a feature inventory (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; represent as a graph of zone nodes with edge constraints; persist in a queryable schema.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a rule-based symbolic representation (e.g., logic rules or ontology) that encodes spatial relationships, safety constraints, and operational policies; derive a queryable model on demand by evaluating rules against sensor observations.

DP4: Establish a real-time streaming pipeline that continuously ingests sensor data, applies feature extraction and filtering, and maintains an incrementally updated spatial model; expose model state via subscription/polling interface for planning/execution.

DP5: Delegate feature capture to a hybrid approach: use pre-defined templates (e.g., site layout blueprints, CAD models) as a baseline; augment with live sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and transient features; merge into a composite model.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Hybrid baseline-augmented model. Establish a composite spatial representation by anchoring to pre-defined worksite templates (blueprints, CAD, or prior surveys) as a static baseline; continuously augment with live multi-modal sensor observations to detect and integrate dynamic features (moving obstacles, temporary hazards, personnel), deviations from baseline, and real-time operational state. Expose the merged model via a structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) that supports both planning and execution consumers. Maintain traceability between baseline and observed features to support safety auditing and policy compliance.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between sensor modality selection and feature extraction logic: mitigated by defining a sensor-agnostic feature schema and abstraction layer.
Dependency on baseline template availability and accuracy: mitigated by graceful degradation (operate with live-only model if baseline unavailable) and versioning.
Coupling between model update frequency and planning/execution cycle time: mitigated by decoupling via asynchronous query interface and explicit freshness metadata.
Policy/safety constraint representation tightly bound to model schema: mitigated by externalizing constraints as a separate, versioned policy layer queryable against the model.
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves loose coupling by separating concerns: baseline (static, pre-computed), live observations (dynamic, sensor-driven), and query interface (consumer-agnostic). The hybrid approach avoids over-dependence on real-time sensing alone (DP1 risk) or manual annotation brittleness (DP2 risk), and defers rule evaluation overhead (DP3) while supporting incremental updates (DP4 benefit).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) are available and reasonably accurate at mission start. Risk: If baseline is stale or unavailable, model degrades to live-only; mitigation: define fallback mode and baseline versioning strategy.

Assumption: Multi-modal sensor suite (vision, range, inertial) is available and calibrated. Risk: Sensor failures or miscalibration corrupt feature extraction; mitigation: implement sensor health monitoring and feature confidence scoring.

Assumption: Feature extraction and fusion algorithms can reliably distinguish dynamic hazards from static baseline. Risk: False positives (noise) or false negatives (missed hazards) compromise safety; mitigation: require human-in-the-loop validation for safety-critical features and define confidence thresholds.

Assumption: Query interface latency and model update frequency meet planning/execution cycle time requirements. Risk: Stale model data leads to unsafe or inefficient decisions; mitigation: define SLA for model freshness and implement priority-based update scheduling.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formalized and queryable. Risk: Policies are ambiguous or incomplete; mitigation: require explicit policy schema and validation against model before execution.

Assumption: Baseline and live observations can be reliably registered in a common coordinate frame. Risk: Registration drift or errors propagate through model; mitigation: implement continuous re-registration and drift detection.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 by explicitly defining: (1) the baseline template schema (geometry, feature types, metadata); (2) the live observation schema and confidence/freshness metadata; (3) the merge/reconciliation logic (conflict resolution, priority rules); (4) the query interface contract (spatial predicates, feature filters, constraint checks); (5) the policy layer schema and binding to model features; (6) the fallback/degradation strategy if baseline or sensors fail. Introduce an IM layer to clarify ownership: baseline management (IM-Baseline), live sensor fusion (IM-Fusion), model storage (IM-Store), query service (IM-Query), policy engine (IM-Policy). This decomposition supports independent development, testing, and evolution of each module while maintaining clear interfaces.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized spatial representation engine that maintains hybrid baseline-augmented models, manages sensor fusion, provides structured query interfaces, and tracks feature traceability for safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, radar, thermal) from Vehicle and Teleoperation to augment baseline model with dynamic features and real-time operational state.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Exposes structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) to planning and execution consumers for mission planning and real-time navigation decisions.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits safety-relevant deviations from baseline, detected hazards, and traceability records to Safety Services for auditing and policy compliance verification.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) from external repositories and synchronizes augmented model state across distributed nodes if multi-agent scenarios apply.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE is the natural home for spatial representation, sensor fusion, and query logic. Baseline management, augmentation pipeline, and traceability tracking are core NGHE responsibilities. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation) and safety alert emission (Safety Services) cross boundaries, both via well-defined interfaces.

Poor fit. Vehicle is a platform/hardware abstraction; it should not own the global spatial model or query logic. Baseline templates, sensor fusion algorithms, and traceability auditing belong in a higher-level planning/perception layer. Placing DP5 here would scatter query consumers (planning, execution) across multiple modules and duplicate baseline logic.

Poor fit. Teleoperation is a control/interface mode, not a data model owner. The hybrid baseline-augmented model must be independent of whether the vehicle is autonomous or teleoperated. Placing DP5 here couples the spatial representation to a specific operational mode and forces duplication if autonomous planning also needs the model.

Weak fit. Safety Services is a consumer of the spatial model (for hazard detection and compliance auditing), not the owner. The baseline-augmented model is a shared asset used by planning, execution, and safety; centralizing it in Safety Services would create a bottleneck and violate separation of concerns. Safety Services should query the model, not maintain it.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP5 (hybrid baseline-augmented model with structured query interface and traceability) is best contained within NGHE Solution because: (1) NGHE is the natural locus for spatial reasoning, sensor fusion, and multi-modal perception; (2) baseline templates and augmentation logic are core NGHE responsibilities; (3) the query interface serves planning and execution, both NGHE consumers; (4) traceability and compliance auditing are NGHE outputs to Safety Services, not Safety Services' internal logic; (5) Vehicle and Teleoperation are lower-level abstractions that should not own global state; (6) diffusion is minimal and confined to well-defined telemetry ingestion and safety alert emission.
containment_risks: Risk: If baseline templates are managed by an external system (e.g., mission planning or fleet management), NGHE must provide a clear interface to ingest and version them. Ensure Network Link interface supports baseline synchronization without tight coupling.

Risk: If multiple consumers (planning, execution, safety) query the model concurrently, NGHE must implement thread-safe or event-driven query semantics. Define query latency and consistency requirements to avoid blocking real-time execution.
Risk: Traceability between baseline and observed features requires persistent logging. Ensure NGHE has sufficient storage and query performance for audit trails, especially in long-duration missions.

Risk: If sensor fusion algorithms are complex or domain-specific (e.g., multi-hypothesis tracking), NGHE may need to delegate to specialized sub-modules. Ensure clear ownership of fusion logic and avoid diffusion to Vehicle or Teleoperation.

Risk: If the baseline model is very large (e.g., high-resolution CAD of a large worksite), NGHE must implement spatial indexing and lazy loading to avoid memory bloat. Coordinate with Network Link to stream baseline data efficiently.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR ('Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model') is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) 'A worksite model/map is generated and available to planning/execution functions for an agreed set of site features' maps to the DP's structured query interface and IM's Tasking output; (2) 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' maps to the DP's traceability and SafetyAlert interface. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution) provides clear observability points: query response times, baseline-augmented model completeness, traceability audit logs, and safety alert emission rates.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Excellent. The FR is unambiguously allocated to NGHE Solution via DP5. The IM boundary owns the hybrid model, sensor fusion pipeline, query logic, and traceability tracking. External interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link inout) are well-defined and decouple NGHE from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. No ambiguity about which module is responsible for model generation, augmentation, or query serving.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 (hybrid baseline-augmented model with structured query interface and traceability) directly and completely addresses the FR. The DP specifies: (1) 'composite spatial representation by anchoring to pre-defined worksite templates' satisfies 'capture...spatial features'; (2) 'continuously augment with live multi-modal sensor observations' satisfies dynamic feature integration; (3) 'expose via structured query interface' satisfies 'machine-readable model' and availability to planning/execution; (4) 'maintain traceability' satisfies safety auditing and policy compliance. No logical gaps or ambiguities between problem statement and solution concept.

diffusion_risks: Baseline template ingestion: If baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) are sourced from external repositories managed by mission planning or fleet management systems, NGHE must provide a versioned, non-blocking Network Link interface. Risk of tight coupling if baseline updates are synchronous or require NGHE to validate schema. Mitigation: Define baseline schema contracts and async push/pull semantics.

Sensor fusion algorithm ownership: If multi-modal sensor fusion (LiDAR, camera, radar, thermal) involves domain-specific algorithms (e.g., multi-hypothesis tracking, occupancy grids, semantic segmentation), NGHE may delegate to specialized sub-modules. Risk of diffusion if fusion logic leaks into Vehicle (raw sensor drivers) or Teleoperation (operator-driven corrections). Mitigation: Encapsulate fusion algorithms within NGHE; expose only augmented feature streams to external consumers.

Concurrent query semantics: If planning, execution, and safety services query the model simultaneously, NGHE must implement thread-safe or event-driven query semantics. Risk of blocking real-time execution if queries are synchronous and baseline model is large. Mitigation: Define query latency SLAs (e.g., <100ms for spatial queries) and implement spatial indexing (quadtree, octree) to avoid full-model scans.

Traceability storage and audit performance: Maintaining persistent logs of baseline vs. observed feature deviations for long-duration missions (hours to days) requires efficient storage and query performance. Risk of NGHE becoming a bottleneck if audit queries are slow or storage grows unbounded. Mitigation: Implement time-windowed or hierarchical traceability logs; define retention policies aligned with safety compliance requirements.

Baseline model size and memory footprint: High-resolution CAD or dense prior surveys of large worksites may exceed available memory. Risk of NGHE failing to load or augment the baseline if spatial indexing is not implemented. Mitigation: Implement lazy loading, spatial partitioning, and streaming baseline data via Network Link; define memory budgets and graceful degradation strategies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment. NGHE Solution is the natural and only appropriate home for DP5. The module containment scores confirm: NGHE 0.92 (excellent fit), Vehicle 0.15 (poor fit), Teleoperation 0.2 (poor fit), Safety Services 0.25 (weak fit). The IM boundary correctly isolates the hybrid baseline-augmented model, sensor fusion, and query logic from lower-level platform abstractions (Vehicle, Teleoperation) and consumer services (Safety Services, planning, execution). All external dependencies are routed through well-defined interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link inout). Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion and safety alert emission cross boundaries, both necessary and appropriate. No evidence of scattered responsibilities or duplicate logic across modules.

recommended_revisions: Formalize baseline template schema and versioning protocol. Define a contract for baseline ingestion via Network Link (e.g., GeoJSON, OGC standards, or proprietary CAD format) and specify how NGHE handles schema evolution, missing features, and baseline conflicts during augmentation.

Specify query interface SLAs and consistency guarantees. Define latency budgets for spatial queries (e.g., <100ms for point-in-polygon, <500ms for constraint checks), consistency model (eventual vs. strong), and handling of concurrent updates from telemetry and baseline synchronization.

Document sensor fusion algorithm ownership and sub-module boundaries. If fusion logic is complex (e.g., Kalman filtering, occupancy grids, semantic segmentation), define which algorithms are NGHE-internal vs. delegated to specialized sub-modules, and ensure clear ownership to prevent diffusion to Vehicle or Teleoperation.

Define traceability audit log structure and retention policy. Specify what baseline-vs-observed deviations are logged (e.g., new obstacles, moved features, policy violations), log granularity (per-feature, per-scan, per-mission), retention duration (aligned with safety compliance), and query performance requirements for audit trails.

Implement spatial indexing and memory management strategy. For large worksites, specify spatial partitioning scheme (quadtree, octree, grid), lazy loading strategy for baseline data, and memory budgets. Define graceful degradation if baseline exceeds available memory (e.g., reduced resolution, spatial windowing).

Clarify SafetyAlert emission criteria and frequency. Define which deviations from baseline trigger safety alerts (e.g., new obstacles within 2m, personnel in restricted zones, policy violations), alert latency requirements, and how NGHE avoids alert flooding during high-frequency sensor updates.

Establish baseline synchronization protocol for multi-agent scenarios. If multiple vehicles or distributed NGHE instances share the same worksite, define how baseline templates and augmented model state are synchronized (e.g., eventual consistency, conflict resolution, version vectors) via Network Link.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence. The FR is well-defined, the DP logically addresses it completely, and the IM boundary is correctly allocated to NGHE Solution with excellent containment (0.92 score). However, the recommended_revisions and diffusion_risks reveal that DP5 contains multiple distinct sub-problems that must be decomposed to ensure architectural clarity, interface contracts, and risk mitigation at the next level. Specifically: (1) baseline template ingestion and schema management is a separable concern requiring its own FR-DP-IM chain; (2) multi-modal sensor fusion algorithm encapsulation is a distinct sub-problem with ownership and diffusion risks; (3) concurrent query semantics and SLA guarantees require dedicated design; (4) traceability audit logging is a separate functional concern with performance and compliance implications; (5) spatial indexing and memory management for large-scale models is a distinct architectural problem. These sub-problems are not yet resolved at the current level and must be pushed down to prevent diffusion, ensure testability, and clarify module boundaries. Safety criticality (high) reinforces the need for explicit decomposition of traceability, query consistency, and alert emission logic. Proceeding to next-level decomposition will resolve ambiguities in baseline synchronization, query latency SLAs, and sensor fusion ownership before detailed design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Ingest, validate, and version baseline worksite templates (CAD, blueprints, prior surveys) from external mission planning systems without blocking real-time augmentation or creating tight coupling.

FR5.2: Fuse multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, radar, thermal) into a unified augmented feature representation while maintaining algorithm encapsulation and preventing diffusion to Vehicle or Teleoperation layers.

FR5.3: Serve spatial queries (point-in-polygon, constraint checks, feature retrieval) to planning, execution, and safety services with latency <100ms for point queries and <500ms for complex constraints, with strong consistency guarantees for safety-critical features.

FR5.4: Maintain and audit traceability logs of baseline-vs-observed feature deviations with configurable granularity, retention policies aligned with safety compliance, and query performance <200ms for audit trail retrieval.
FR5.5: Implement spatial indexing (quadtree/octree) and memory management to support large-scale worksite models (>1GB baseline data) with graceful degradation and lazy loading via Network Link.

FR5.6: Emit safety alerts for policy violations and anomalies (new obstacles, personnel in restricted zones, baseline deviations) with latency <500ms and configurable thresholds to prevent alert flooding during high-frequency sensor updates.

revise.why: No revisions needed at current level. FR, DP, and IM all pass gates with high confidence and clear allocation. Recommended_revisions are design-level clarifications (schema contracts, SLAs, sub-module boundaries) that belong in next-level decomposition, not current-level revision.

assumptions: Baseline templates are sourced from external mission planning systems and delivered via Network Link with defined schema contracts (e.g., GeoJSON, OGC standards, or proprietary CAD format).
Multi-modal sensor fusion algorithms (Kalman filtering, occupancy grids, semantic segmentation) are encapsulated within NGHE Solution and do not leak to Vehicle or Teleoperation layers.
Query latency SLAs (<100ms for point queries, <500ms for complex constraints) are achievable with spatial indexing (quadtree/octree) and are acceptable to planning/execution consumers.
Traceability audit logs are retained for mission duration (hours to days) and queried post-mission for safety compliance; real-time audit query performance is not a hard constraint.
Worksite models may exceed available memory (>1GB baseline data); lazy loading and spatial partitioning are required for large-scale deployments.
Safety alert emission thresholds and latency requirements (<500ms) are defined by Safety Services and are achievable without blocking real-time augmentation.
Multi-agent scenarios (multiple vehicles sharing worksite) require eventual consistency for baseline synchronization; strong consistency is not required for non-safety-critical features.
open_risks: Baseline template schema evolution: If mission planning systems update baseline schema (e.g., new feature types, coordinate systems), NGHE must handle schema versioning and backward compatibility without blocking augmentation. Risk: tight coupling or data loss if schema contracts are not formalized.

Sensor fusion algorithm performance: Multi-modal fusion (LiDAR + camera + radar + thermal) may exceed real-time budgets if algorithms are not optimized. Risk: augmentation latency exceeds planning/execution requirements, causing stale model state.

Query consistency under concurrent updates: If baseline synchronization and telemetry augmentation occur concurrently, queries may return inconsistent state (e.g., baseline feature moved but augmented feature not yet updated). Risk: planning/execution decisions based on stale or conflicting model state.

Traceability storage growth: Long-duration missions (>8 hours) with high-frequency sensor updates (>10Hz) may generate audit logs exceeding storage budgets. Risk: NGHE storage exhaustion or query performance degradation.

Spatial indexing overhead: Building and maintaining spatial indices (quadtree/octree) for large models may consume significant CPU and memory. Risk: augmentation latency increases if indexing is not incremental or lazy.

Alert flooding: High-frequency sensor updates may trigger repeated safety alerts for the same anomaly (e.g., persistent obstacle). Risk: alert desensitization or planning/execution paralysis if alerts are not deduplicated or throttled.

Baseline synchronization in multi-agent scenarios: If multiple vehicles share worksite and baseline is updated by one vehicle, other vehicles may operate on stale baseline. Risk: inconsistent planning decisions or safety violations if synchronization is not timely.
evidence_needed: Baseline template schema specification (GeoJSON, OGC, or proprietary CAD format) with versioning and backward compatibility rules.
Sensor fusion algorithm performance benchmarks (latency, CPU, memory) for each modality (LiDAR, camera, radar, thermal) and fusion method (Kalman, occupancy grid, semantic segmentation).
Query latency measurements for representative worksite models (small <100MB, medium 100MB-1GB, large >1GB) with spatial indexing (quadtree/octree) and concurrent query loads.
Traceability audit log size estimates for mission durations (1h, 4h, 8h, 24h) with high-frequency sensor updates (10Hz, 20Hz, 50Hz) and retention policies.
Memory profiling and spatial partitioning strategy for large-scale baseline models (>1GB) with lazy loading and graceful degradation.
Safety alert emission criteria and frequency analysis: which deviations trigger alerts, alert latency requirements, and deduplication/throttling thresholds to prevent flooding.
Multi-agent baseline synchronization protocol specification (eventual consistency, conflict resolution, version vectors) and synchronization latency measurements.
